content.heading: The Project
content.body: A complete redesign of a 280 sqm penthouse for a private client relocating to Kampala. The existing interior was dated and compartmentalised; the brief was to open it up, modernise the material palette, and create a home that balances daily life with the ability to entertain at a high level.

content.caption: The reconfigured open plan connects kitchen, dining, and terrace in one continuous space.

items.heading: Before
items.body: Five small rooms, low ceilings, and a kitchen isolated from the main living area. The previous owners had closed off the terrace access entirely, severing the apartment from its panoramic city views.

items.heading: After
items.body: Two structural walls removed, the kitchen relocated and opened to dining, and full-height sliding doors reinstated to the wrap-around terrace. The penthouse now reads as a single, breathing space with the city as its backdrop.

content.heading: Finish Palette
content.body: Brushed concrete floors, white-oak joinery, linen-blend upholstery, and accents in aged brass. A deliberate restraint in the palette allows the views and light to carry the space.
